name: Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ventosa traverse a region characterized by varied terrain, including hilly and mountainous landscapes. Routes often wind through dense forests and along river valleys, providing diverse riding environments. The area offers a mix of elevations, from moderate climbs to more demanding ascents, catering to different skill levels. This topography makes Ventosa a suitable destination for a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Ventosa The most popular mountain bike trail is Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular, an 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside and reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Moncalvillo mountain range. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto de Moncalvillo Loop, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through the higher elevations of Moncalvillo, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Ventosa is defined by hilly terrain, dense woodlands, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Ventosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ventosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ventosa?
A: Ventosa offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ventosa?
A: Yes, Ventosa has several options for beginners and families. There are 14 easy mountain bike trails, often winding through scenic river valleys and less challenging terrain. These routes provide a great way to enjoy the outdoors without overly demanding ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ventosa?
A: Mountain bike trails in Ventosa feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter hilly and mountainous landscapes, dense woodlands, and scenic river valleys. The region offers a mix of elevations, from moderate climbs to more demanding ascents, providing varied riding experiences.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ventosa?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Ventosa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655115/ventosa-dehesa-de-hornos-embalse-de-valbornedo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the local countryside and reservoir.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Ventosa?
A: Experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655187/moncalvillo-btt-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 43.2 km path with significant elevation gain through the Moncalvillo mountain range. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993876776/alto-de-moncalvillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through higher elevations of Moncalvillo.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ventosa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ventosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the dense woodlands, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region around Ventosa offers several rewarding viewpoints. You can find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;LasNeveras&lt;/a&gt; and the general higher elevations of Moncalvillo, which provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscapes and mountain ranges.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Ventosa?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can explore natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6187988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards Between Logroño and Nájera&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque landscapes. The region's diverse topography also includes river valleys and dense forests, providing a rich natural environment.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can combine your ride with visits to historical and cultural sites. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poyo de Roldán Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Former hospital of San Juan de Acre&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by reservoirs or water features?
A: Absolutely. Several routes incorporate water features, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655115/ventosa-dehesa-de-hornos-embalse-de-valbornedo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which explores the local reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Embalse de Valbornedo loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Embalse de Valbornedo.

Q: Are there any routes that feature unique man-made monuments?
A: Yes, you can find routes that pass by distinctive man-made monuments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31573742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Sotés&lt;/a&gt; includes a sighting of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4509944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull&lt;/a&gt;, a well-known Spanish landmark.
tips: Beautiful Dehesa near Daroca and Hornos de Moncalvillo
A quiet place, with the possibility of seeing varied fauna. It is worth visiting the vineyards and paths around the reservoir. Afterwards you can visit some of the potteries in the area.
Drinking water fountain at the foot of the road to Navarrete. Opposite is the cemetery whose door is that of the old pilgrims' hospital. The road to Santiago passes through there.

Sotes is the classic Riojan crossroads of road and mountain bike routes. It is a welcoming town to make a stopover and among its buildings stands out the Renaissance church of San Martín from the 17th century. It is elevated above the Ebro valley so you can enjoy the views as you pass.
